game_name: Shadow Dancer: The Secret of Shinobi

danger_signals.visual_form: Union Lizard cult members in dark ninja attire positioned throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to player; hostiles will engage on sight

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies positioned behind boxes and cover, reloading weapons after firing
danger_signals.meaning: Ranged threat; projectile attacks incoming; enemies use tactical positioning

danger_signals.visual_form: Third area of each stage features confrontation chamber with end boss
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger; major threat encounter ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: New York City rendered in destroyed state with debris and structural damage
danger_signals.meaning: World state of chaos; prior catastrophic event by Union Lizard; active conflict zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostages scattered throughout levels requiring rescue
danger_signals.meaning: Occupied territory; Union Lizard control established; civilians in peril

safety_signals.visual_form: Male hostages grant bonus points and extra lives; female hostages enhance protagonist attacks
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward objective; resource acquisition; temporary power enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Dog Yamato accompanies player, barks toward enemies when attack meter filled
safety_signals.meaning: Allied presence; tactical advantage available; companion AI responds to threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Between-round stage where protagonist jumps from building to eliminate 50 enemy ninjas
safety_signals.meaning: Structured safety check; controlled enemy encounter; opportunity for life/point gain

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Giant reptilian demon worship symbol (Sauros as faction deity)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dark ninja attire; armed soldier variants with ranged weapons; cult members use tactical positioning
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ruined New York City transformed to cult stronghold; prisoner encampments; reptilian demon architecture implied in final stage (Sauros throne room)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ninja warrior identity; ninjutsu attack capability; lineage (Hayate son of Joe Musashi or Joe Musashi himself)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Playable character wields shuriken and ninjutsu; uses dog companion Yamato as tactical asset
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Emerges from hiding; infiltrates ruined city; rescues hostages; confronts cult across five stages

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Union Lizard occupation of New York City (1997)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: City rendered in ruined state with widespread destruction; architectural damage throughout levels; debris-filled environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy ninjas actively patrol and occupy territory; hostages imprisoned throughout stages; cult members use cover and tactical positioning; demon worship symbolized via Sauros as final boss

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hostages scattered as rescue objectives across four main stages; male and female hostage variants with different visual identification
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Protagonist prioritizes rescue mission; completing stages requires hostage liberation; rescued hostages provide mechanical benefits (points, lives, attack enhancement)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Protagonist character animation shows skilled movement and combat readiness; ninjutsu attack capability indicated through action animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player infiltrates enemy territory across five stages; uses dog companion as tactical tool; applies ninjutsu for enhanced combat; bonus points awarded for stage completion without ninjutsu use (suggesting strategic choice value)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First four stages: three areas each with progressive enemy density; fifth stage: five-room sequence leading to Sauros throne
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy placement varies by difficulty setting; boss encounters escalate in threat level; final stage represents cult stronghold heart; Sauros as final boss embodies reptilian demon worship central to Union Lizard ideology

environmental_storytelling.example: New York City rendered as ruins by Union Lizard onslaught; visual decay communicates prior catastrophic conflict and ongoing occupation
Enemy soldiers duck behind boxes to reload weapons after firing; indicates organized military discipline within cult structure; shows technological sophistication despite demonic cult identity
Scattered hostages throughout stages visually mark Union Lizard control zones; varying hostage types (male/female) provide mechanical differentiation suggesting captured population diversity

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss arena design as faction representation
environmental_storytelling.example: Final stage progression to Sauros throne room; architectural style shift signals entry into cult's sacred/administrative space; demon boss as faction spiritual center
Ninja emerges from hiding; moves through hostile territory with only dog companion; visual composition emphasizes solitary infiltration mission against organized occupation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follows protagonist; does not attack without player input
Barks indicate enemy proximity and attack readiness when player holds attack button to fill meter
Fires projectiles; uses cover (boxes) for tactical positioning; reloads weapons after firing sequences
Eliminated from level; contribution to bonus stage enemy count; scoring mechanics apply
Grant bonus points and occasional extra lives; mechanical reward for rescue objective
Enhance protagonist attacks until next stage or player death; temporary power-up effect
Bonus points awarded based on completion time and ninjutsu usage choice; rewards efficiency and tactical restraint
Represents Union Lizard reptilian demon theology; occupies throne room in stage five; final confrontation after infiltrating cult stronghold

notes: Shadow Dancer: The Secret of Shinobi uses environmental destruction and occupation as primary world-state indicators. The ruined New York setting immediately communicates prior catastrophic conflict. Faction identity centers on Union Lizard's demonic reptilian cult theology, manifested through enemy types and final boss design. Danger signals emphasize tactical enemy behavior (cover use, weapon reloading) rather than just visual appearance, suggesting organized military discipline within the cult. The canine companion serves dual mechanical and thematic roles: gameplay tool and symbolic ally in isolated protagonist narrative. Hostage rescue mechanics directly embed story objective into gameplay, with hostage variants (male/female) providing both narrative population diversity and distinct mechanical benefits. Bonus stage design creates controlled narrative checkpoint where enemy elimination becomes ritualized sport-like encounter. Difficulty settings and ninjutsu usage scoring mechanics suggest intentional design of multiple completion philosophies. Visual composition emphasizes protagonist solitude against organized opposition, reinforcing infiltration-mission narrative despite arcade-derived platform gameplay structure.
